rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» - 6 стихир влкмч., Сл.: влкмч, І нині: хрестобогород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар влкмч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря влкмч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. влкмч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар влкмч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря влкмч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і влкмч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний влкмч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар влкмч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря влкмч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. влкмч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – влкмч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Кор. 137 зач.; 7, 12-24. Єв. – Мт. 60 зач.; 14, 35 – 15, 11.

troparion_en.text: Your holy martyr Procopius, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions save our souls!

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the highest...”)
Set ablaze by divine zeal for Christ and protected by the might of the Cross, / you cast down the insolence and boldness of the enemy, Procopius. / You exalted the honorable Church, / excelling in faith and giving light to us all!

matched_oca_saint: Greatmartyr Procopius of Caesarea, in Palestine

oca_saints.name: “Our Lady of Sitka” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Today, like the morning sun rising over us, / your all-honored icon enlightens the world with rays of mercy / and our land reverently receives it / as a divine gift from on high, / glorifying you, O Birthgiver of God, Our Lady of Sitka, / with joy magnifying Christ our God Who was born of you. / Pray to Him O Lady Mary, Queen and Theotokos / that all cities and lands be protected from our enemies, / and that they will be saved who in faith venerate your most pure icon / that has come to dwell with us, O Virgin Mother, / who shows us the way to Christ.

kontakion.text: O Mother of God, chosen from all generations to be the protectress of the Christian people; / we offer you songs of thanksgiving for your wonderworking Icon that has come to Alaska. / You are a fountain of mercy and help all who seek refuge in you. / Defend us in all afflictions, necessities and tribulations that we may cry to you: / Rejoice, zealous defender of the Orthodox faithful in America.

oca_saints.name: Greatmartyr Procopius of Caesarea, in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Procopius, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the highest...”)
Set ablaze by divine zeal for Christ and protected by the might of the Cross, / you cast down the insolence and boldness of the enemy, Procopius. / You exalted the honorable Church, / excelling in faith and giving light to us all!

oca_saints.name: Kazan Icons of the Mother of God in Kazan, St. Petersburg, and Moscow

troparion.text: O fervent intercessor, Mother of the Lord Most High,  / you pray to your Son, Christ our God for all, and save all those who have recourse to your powerful protection.  / Defend us, O Lady, Queen, and Sovereign Lady,  / when we who are burdened with many sins, stand before you in misfortune, sorrow, and sickness, / praying to you with tender souls and a contrite heart,  / shedding tears before this, your most pure Icon, and having steadfast hope in you who deliver us from all evils.  / Grant us what is profitable for our souls and save all, O Virgin Theotokos,  / for you are the Divine protection of your God-fearing servants.

kontakion.text: Let us run to the quiet and good refuge,  / and to the speedy helper, ardent and eager to save all. / Let us run to the Virgin’s protection and hasten to repentance; / for the Most Pure Theotokos grants us unfailing mercy as she comes to help all, / delivering her devout and God-fearing servants from great troubles and evils.

oca_saints.name: “Tenderness” Icon of the Mother of God in Novgorod

troparion.text: Let all of us, who are burdened with sins, fall on our knees before the Theotokos,  /  bowing before her wonderworking Icon of Tenderness,  /  kissing it and crying aloud with tears: / “O Sovereign Lady, accept the prayer of your unworthy servants,  /  and grant to us who ask, your great mercy.”

kontakion.text: We have no other help, we have no other hope but you, O Lady.  /  Help us, for in you do we hope,  /  and of you do we boast, for we are your servants.  /  Let us not be put to shame.

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyrs Epictetus and Astion, at Halmyris

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, / always act with kindness towards us; / take not Your mercy from us, / but guide our lives in peace / through the prayers of the Martyrs Epictetus and Astion.

kontakion.text: As venerable and proven ascetics, as honorable and renowned martyrs,  / and as models of the desert life, O most exalted Epictetus and Astion, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Theophilus the Myrrhgusher of Macedonia

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Theophilus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Tambov

troparion.text: O fervent intercessor, Mother of the Lord Most High,  / you pray to your Son, Christ our God for all, and save all those who have recourse to your powerful protection.  / Defend us, O Lady, Queen, and Sovereign Lady,  / when we who are burdened with many sins, stand before you in misfortune, sorrow, and sickness, / praying to you with tender souls and a contrite heart,  / shedding tears before this, your most pure Icon, and having steadfast hope in you who deliver us from all evils.  / Grant us what is profitable for our souls and save all, O Virgin Theotokos,  / for you are the Divine protection of your God-fearing servants.

kontakion.text: Let us run to the quiet and good refuge,  / and to the speedy helper, ardent and eager to save all. / Let us run to the Virgin’s protection and hasten to repentance; / for the Most Pure Theotokos grants us unfailing mercy as she comes to help all, / delivering her devout and God-fearing servants from great troubles and evils.

oca_saints.name: Royal Martyr Mirdat, King of Kartli

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Mirdat, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Mirdat;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Kazan-Yaroslavl Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: O fervent intercessor, Mother of the Lord Most High,  / you pray to your Son, Christ our God for all, and save all those who have recourse to your powerful protection.  / Defend us, O Lady, Queen, and Sovereign Lady,  / when we who are burdened with many sins, stand before you in misfortune, sorrow, and sickness, / praying to you with tender souls and a contrite heart,  / shedding tears before this, your most pure Icon, and having steadfast hope in you who deliver us from all evils.  / Grant us what is profitable for our souls and save all, O Virgin Theotokos,  / for you are the Divine protection of your God-fearing servants.

kontakion.text: Let us run to the quiet and good refuge,  / and to the speedy helper, ardent and eager to save all. / Let us run to the Virgin’s protection and hasten to repentance; / for the Most Pure Theotokos grants us unfailing mercy as she comes to help all, / delivering her devout and God-fearing servants from great troubles and evils.
